summary: Define terrain-route-node strategic map authority and query boundaries
acceptance_criteria: Define serializable strategic terrain authority types for TerrainPatch, BarrierChannel, StrategicNode, RouteCorridor, and DistrictGovernanceFootprint overlay.

Explicitly supersede polygon-first governance cells as the player-facing spatial substrate; governance footprints remain overlays for control, obligations, appointments, taxation, legitimacy, and postwar governance.

Define deterministic terrain-route-node query surfaces for hit testing, path preview, route explanation, AI reasons, blockers, unknown state, capacity, bottleneck, season, terrain, risk, and governance consequences.
Define spatial-query priority from actionable corridors/nodes/crossings/blockers to terrain explanation layers and only then district governance overlays.
Permit replacement of the rejected drawing/spatial-index model in later renderer work; do not require compatibility with polygon-first hit testing except as an explicit migration bridge.

No authoritative reachability, route cost, AI reason, save compatibility, or default UI behavior may derive from hidden grid, hidden lattice, hex axial/cube coordinates, sequential ids, centroid guesses, bounding-box adjacency, or renderer-only lines.
Define save/content hash behavior for strategic terrain schema changes: migrate only when explicit and safe, otherwise structured rejection without silent fallback.
Keep formulas at intent level: every advantage must carry local cost/dependency/counter and no hidden global penalty, fixed culture/ethnicity buff, or person total-stat shortcut.
required_tests: focused deterministic query tests for layer priority, terrain-route-node path reasons, stable tie-breaks, and strategic terrain hash behavior
negative tests proving district governance adjacency, polygon centroid proximity, hidden grid/lattice ids, and renderer-only lines cannot create route reachability
notes: This task becomes eligible only after M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-RECOVERY-001 receives systems/QA acceptance.
ADR-010 remains anti-grid negative proof; ADR-011 is the player-facing strategic terrain target.

This task must not implement client rendering, content fixture migration, high-fidelity art, formal GIS data, production cultural assets, server/multiplayer, telemetry, Rust/WASM, or manual node battle scope.
next_action: Open PR, wait for CI, merge to main, then close M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001 and continue to the next READY strategic terrain task.
